text: Ever wonder why things fall apart? It’s called entropy. In physics, entropy measures disorder. The universe naturally favors chaos over neatness. Imagine a tidy room. Over time, it becomes a chaotic mess. That’s entropy in action. The second law of thermodynamics states that total entropy can never decrease. It’s a one-way street. This principle isn’t just theory. It drives engines, powers reactions, and defines the flow of time itself. Want to dive deeper into the universe’s secrets? Hit subscribe and explore with me.
